channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: French far right works to turn election win into power. Its rivals want to stop it in decisive vote
item.description: PARIS (AP) — With the ultimate outcome still up in the air, France's fiercely anti-immigration National Rally and opponents of the long-taboo far-right party scrambled on Monday to capitalize on an indecisive first round of voting the day before in surprise legislative elections.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, SYLVIE CORBET and BARBARA SURK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Turkey's president accuses opposition of stoking racism after anti-Syrian rioting erupts
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan accused opposition parties of stoking xenophobia and racism on Monday, a day after residents in a neighborhood in central Turkey set Syrian-owned shops on fire.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: California prisoner who spent 13 years on the run after escaping prison camp is recaptured
item.description: Eduardo Hernandez, who has spent nearly 13 years on the run after escaping a prison camp in California's Bay Area, has been tracked down in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gathered at Camp David, Biden's family tells him to stay in the presidential race and keep fighting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden's family used a gathering at Camp David to urge him to stay in the race and keep fighting despite his dreadful debate performance, and some members criticized how his staff prepared him for the faceoff, according to four people familiar with the discussions.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hurricane Beryl takes aim at southeastern Caribbean as a powerful Category 4 storm
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Hurricane Beryl began pounding the southeast Caribbean on Monday as a powerful Category 4 storm after becoming the earliest storm of that strength to form in the Atlantic, fueled by record warm waters.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Boeing agrees to acquire Spirit AeroSystem
item.description: (WJET/WFXP) — Boeing announced on Monday that they have agreed to a definitive merger agreement to acquire Spirit AreoSystems. "We believe this deal is in the best interest of the flying public, our airline customers, the employees of Spirit and Boeing, our shareholders and the country more broadly.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Dangerous Hurricane Beryl restrengthens to Category 4 off Grenada
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Beryl has restrengthened into an extremely dangerous Category 4 hurricane. The National Hurricane Center in Miami said the storm was located about 70 miles (125 kilometers) east of Grenada on Monday morning and had maximum sustained winds of 130 mph (215 kph.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Inside VidCon 2024: Here's the secret to becoming a social media star
item.description: VidCon is a place where creators, their fans, brands and merch collide. "Hollywood is no longer just Hollywood," explains Sarah Tortoreti, VidCon's SVP of Marketing and Communications.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: After 32 years as a progressive voice for LGBTQ Jews, Rabbi Sharon Kleinbaum heads into retirement
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For more than three decades, Rabbi Sharon Kleinbaum has led the nation’s largest LGBTQ+ synagogue through the myriad ups and downs of the modern gay-rights movement — through the AIDS crisis, the murder of Matthew Shepard, the historic civil-rights advances that included marriage equ
item.dc:creator: KEN SWEET, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecutors target West Texas meth dealers
item.description: Separate grand juries this week charged four men and three women with conspiring to distribute methamphetamine in the oil-rich Midland, Texas, region.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Live updates: Nation braces for Supreme Court’s Trump immunity decision
item.description: The Supreme Court is expected to issue its highly anticipated opinion on former President Trump's claims he has presidential immunity from criminal prosecution on its final day of releasing opinions Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: US Supreme Court Latest: Court expected to rule on Trump immunity case as end of term nears
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court justices will soon take the bench to release their last few opinions of the term, including a closely watched case: Whether former President Donald Trump has immunity from criminal prosecution for his role in the Jan. 6, 2021, riot at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Voters kick all the Republican women out of the South Carolina Senate
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — The only three Republican women in the South Carolina Senate took on their party and stopped a total abortion ban from passing in their state last year. In return, they lost their jobs. Voters removed Sens.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court decision on Trump immunity expected Monday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court's final day of the term will be Monday, when it issues a critical decision on whether former President Donald Trump has immunity from prosecution for his actions related to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden campaign email details how to defend president’s 'rough' debate performance and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jocelyn Nungaray murder: Texas county dubbed 'sanctuary for criminals' as Dem DA tries to shift blame
item.description: Harris County District Attorney Kim Ogg blamed the "broken" immigration system as two illegal immigrants were accused of killing 12-year-old Jocelyn Nungaray in Houston, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah family desperate for answers after man vanishes before lunch date with mom: 'He matters'
item.description: Justin Hooiman vanished after making plans to meet his mom for lunch in Salt Lake City in November 2017 – now police are looking into a potential homicide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican JD Vance journeys from 'Hillbilly Elegy' memoirist to US senator to VP contender
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — It was March 2022 and Senate candidate JD Vance was standing under hot lights in a Cleveland television studio debating four fellow Republicans on whether the U.S. should support a no-fly zone over Ukraine, not a month into its grinding war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Father-son duo hits $1 million jackpot in Kentucky: 'I ain't ever eating bologna again'
item.description: “I said [to the clerk], ‘If I hit this million dollars, I ain’t ever eating bologna again,’” the dad said.
item.dc:creator: Madylin Goins, NewsNation

item.title: Trump immunity decision in balance as Supreme Court caps term
item.description: The Supreme Court is set to rule on Monday whether former President Trump has immunity from criminal prosecution, a monumental decision that comes just days after the court dropped a decision throwing into doubt charges against hundreds of Jan. 6 rioters and Trump himself.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 2.8 million student loan borrowers won't owe a payment in July: Here's why
item.description: About 3 million student loan borrowers will be placed in forbeareance in July, despite two federal injunctions that came down last week.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Tropical Storm Chris forms over Gulf, third named storm of 2024 hurricane season: NHC
item.description: The National Hurricane Center confirmed Sunday that a tropical depression has formed over the southwestern Gulf of Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3 million student loan borrowers won't owe a payment in July: Here's why
item.description: About 3 million student loan borrowers will be placed in forbeareance in July, despite two federal injunctions that came down last week.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: US wants Boeing to plead guilty to fraud over fatal crashes, lawyers say
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department plans to propose that Boeing plead guilty to criminal fraud in connection with two deadly plane crashes involving its 737 Max jetliners.
item.dc:creator: David Koenig, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gymnast Shilese Jones pulls out of U.S. Olympic trials with leg injury
item.description: Gymnast Shilese Jones pulled out of the U.S. Olympic trials, one day after injuring her left knee while training on the vault.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Teen with pellet gun fatally shot by police: Body cam footage released as community calls for accountability
item.description: Police say the 13-year-old aimed an object - later identified as a pellet gun - at them before he was fatally shot. The boy's family is now calling for the officers to be "put in jail.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Donovan, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Having a child? These are the popular baby-naming trends to know — or avoid
item.description: Couples struggling to find the perfect name for their baby have plenty of resources to help them gather a list of possible choices. But as it just so happens, many of these parents-to-be are seeking the same types of names.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Missing mom, 2 daughters may have been 'coerced to travel to Mexico': Kansas authorities
item.description: Investigators believe all three may have been coerced into traveling to "a religious rehabilitation facility" in Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Carina Branson, KRON4

item.title: Evacuation orders lifted for some Arizona residents forced from their homes days ago by a wildfire
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Evacuation orders in Arizona have been lifted for some residents of northeast Scottsdale, days after they were forced from their homes by a wildfire, authorities said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fire captain deployed to Ground Zero on 9/11 dies in Colorado rafting mishap
item.description: A Utah fire captain who was deployed to Ground Zero after the 9/11 terrorist attacks was killed in a rafting accident in Colorado last week, officials have confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KRON4

item.title: Hurricane Beryl becomes 'extremely dangerous' Category 4 storm
item.description: Hurricane Beryl strengthened into what experts called an "extremely dangerous” Category 4 storm as it approaches the southeast Caribbean.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why some ice creams are sold by the 'squround' and some aren’t
item.description: Ice cream is frequently sold by the pint, the cone, the sandwich or — somewhat less often — the taco. But you buy it by the "squround" too, even if you don't realize it.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Which burger chain has the most expensive prices?
item.description: Unfortunately for the casual fast food fan, prices have risen by a tremendous amount over the last two years.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 11 injured after escalator accelerates at Milwaukee Brewers ballpark
item.description: Six people were hospitalized and five others were treated at the Milwaukee ballpark after an escalator malfunctioned and had "an increased downward speed.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Is there a right way to exit an airplane?
item.description: The most efficient way to disembark an airplane tends to conflict with what most travelers consider to be most polite.
item.dc:creator: Sally French , Fox 5

item.title: American and British voters share deep roots. In 2024, they distrust their own leaders, too
item.description: British and American voters are going to the polls the same year for the first time in more than three decades.
item.dc:creator: Laurie Kellman And Christine Fernando, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Animal rescuers try to keep dozens of dolphins away from Cape Cod shallows after mass stranding
item.description: WELLFLEET, Mass. (AP) — Animal rescuers were trying to keep dozens of dolphins away from shallow waters around Cape Cod on Saturday after 125 of the creatures stranded themselves a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
